jobs.title: Backend Data Engineer
jobs.description: Description Backend / Data Engineer (TypeScript & PostgreSQL) Job Type: Full-Time Summary The Backend / Data Engineer is responsible for designing, developing, and optimizing scalable backend systems that support VRC’s operational and business objectives. This role will serve as a technical expert and strategic partner by building data-driven architectures, improving system performance, ensuring data integrity, and supporting enterprise-level integrations. The Backend / Data Engineer will play a critical role in shaping the flow, transformation, and consumption of data across the organization while collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver reliable, scalable, and high-performing solutions. Essential Functions Architectural Data Flow & Backend Development Responsible for designing and implementing backend systems where architecture is driven by the efficient flow and transformation of data. Develop robust, scalable, and type-safe services and APIs using TypeScript and Node.js while ensuring system reliability and maintainability. Stream Processing & Event-Driven Architecture Design, implement, and optimize Apache Kafka streams and event-driven systems. Ensure high-throughput processing, reliable messaging, scalable topic modeling, and seamless data movement across enterprise platforms. Database Design & Performance Optimization Manage and optimize PostgreSQL databases with a focus on schema design, indexing strategies, query execution plans, partitioning, vacuuming, and low-latency data access. Support large-scale datasets and maintain high system performance. Cross-Functional Collaboration & Technical Support Partner closely with product teams, leadership, and engineering teams to translate business requirements into technical solutions. Participate in code reviews, troubleshooting, mentoring, and knowledge-sharing initiatives while supporting continuous process and system improvements. Enterprise Integration & System Scalability Support integrations between backend systems and enterprise platforms, including CRM and ERP systems. Contribute to scalable architecture decisions that align with organizational growth and long-term technology strategies. Competencies Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Experience designing scalable, data-oriented backend architectures Ability to troubleshoot complex system performance and data flow issues Detail-oriented work style with strong organizational skills Excellent verbal and written communication skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively within cross-functional teams Strong understanding of event-driven architecture and distributed systems Ability to manage multiple priorities and technical initiatives simultaneously Commitment to clean, scalable, and maintainable code standards Strong mentorship and collaborative engineering mindset Requirements Technical Skills & Requirements Advanced experience with TypeScript, Node.js, PostgreSQL, and Apache Kafka Strong understanding of PostgreSQL internals, performance tuning, indexing, partitioning, and relational modeling Experience building enterprise-grade APIs and backend services Knowledge of stream processing, producer/consumer configurations, and event-driven architectures Experience with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and Agile/Scrum development methodologies Familiarity with Docker and Kubernetes preferred Experience with React to support frontend collaboration preferred Experience integrating with Salesforce, CRM, ERP, or enterprise platforms preferred Logistics, inventory management, or operational systems experience preferred Work Environment This position operates in a hybrid work environment and may require collaboration with cross-functional teams across multiple locations. Physical Demands While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit, communicate, and work on a computer for extended periods of time. The employee may occasionally be required to stand, walk, or lift office materials up to 15 pounds. Education & Eligibility Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related technical field preferred Extensive professional experience building backend systems and enterprise-grade applications required Strong hands-on experience with PostgreSQL, Apache Kafka, and TypeScript required Experience supporting scalable data architectures and high-volume datasets preferred Experience working within Agile/Scrum environments preferred Why Join VRC? Opportunity to help shape enterprise-level data architecture and backend strategy Tech-forward environment utilizing modern technologies and scalable infrastructure Collaborative engineering culture focused on innovation, mentorship, and technical excellence Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package Opportunity to solve meaningful operational and logistics-focused technology challenges Other Duties Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee.

jobs.title: Data Engineer
jobs.description: Job Description Job Summary: (Data Engineer) Skillset Hands-on experience with GCP (BigQuery, Composer, Dataproc, Dataflow, Pub/Sub) Working knowledge of Databricks for data processing and pipelines Strong programming skills in SQL and PySpark Experience with Oracle and SQL Server databases Understanding of data modelling (dimensional / medallion basics) Experience in batch pipelines and exposure to streaming Familiarity with data ingestion, ETL/ELT processes Basic understanding of data quality and validation Exposure to Airflow/Composer and scheduling Working knowledge of Git and CI/CD Detailed Responsibilities Develop and maintain batch and streaming data pipelines Build SQL and PySpark transformations for large datasets Ingest data from multiple sources into BigQuery/cloud platforms Implement data transformation logic as per requirements Work with teams to consume curated datasets Develop workflows using Cloud Composer (Airflow) Perform data validation and reconciliation Optimise queries and pipelines for performance Support migration of legacy ETL jobs to cloud Troubleshoot pipeline and performance issues Participate in testing, UAT, and deployment Document pipelines, transformations, and sources Collaborate with analytics and reporting teams Soft Skills Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent communication and stakeholder management Leadership and mentoring capabilities Attention to detail and quality-focused mindset Collaboration and teamwork Time management and prioritization Ability to work in Agile and fast-paced environments Qualifications Bachelor's Range Of Year Experience-Min Year 1 Range Of Year Experience-Max Year 4

jobs.title: Data Engineer
jobs.description: Job Description Must Have Technical/Functional Skills Python, Glue ETL, Attunity, Pyspark, AWS (S3, IAM, Lambda, Cloud Formation) Roles & Responsibilities Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL/data integration pipelines using AWS Glue, PySpark, and Python. Develop and optimize data transformation workflows to process large datasets from multiple source systems. Utilize Attunity for real-time and batch data replication between on-premises and cloud environments. Build and manage data lakes and storage solutions using Amazon S3, ensuring data quality, security, and governance. Implement serverless data processing solutions using AWS Lambda and integrate with other AWS services. Configure and manage IAM roles, policies, and permissions to ensure secure access to AWS resources. Automate infrastructure provisioning and deployment using AWS CloudFormation. Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize ETL jobs, data pipelines, and cloud resources for performance and reliability. Collaborate with business, analytics, and application teams to gather requirements and deliver data solutions. Support CI/CD processes, perform code reviews, and maintain technical documentation following best practices. Generic Managerial Skills, If any Planning & Execution – Effectively prioritize tasks, estimate effort, and ensure timely delivery of project milestones while balancing multiple commitments. Stakeholder Management – Build strong relationships with business teams, clients, and leadership through proactive communication and expectation management. Communication – Convey technical and non-technical information clearly through meetings, presentations, documentation, and status updates. Collaboration – Foster teamwork across cross-functional groups, encourage knowledge sharing, and contribute to a positive team environment. Decision Making – Analyze options, assess risks, and make informed decisions that align with both technical and business objectives. Leadership & Ownership – Take accountability for deliverables, drive initiatives proactively, and lead by example to achieve team goals. Risk & Issue Management – Identify potential risks early, address blockers proactively, and implement mitigation strategies to ensure smooth project execution. Customer & Business Focus – Understand customer needs and business priorities, ensuring technical solutions deliver measurable value. Mentoring & Coaching – Support team growth through guidance, code reviews, knowledge sharing, and skill development initiatives. Project & Change Management – Adapt effectively to evolving requirements, manage priorities during change, and ensure successful delivery outcomes. TCS Employee Benefits Summary Discretionary Annual Incentive. Comprehensive Medical Coverage: Medical & Health, Dental & Vision, Disability Planning & Insurance, Pet Insurance Plans. Family Support: Maternal & Parental Leaves. Insurance Options: Auto & Home Insurance, Identity Theft Protection. Convenience & Professional Growth: Commuter Benefits & Certification & Training Reimbursement. Time Off: Vacation, Time Off, Sick Leave & Holidays. Legal & Financial As sistance: Legal Assistance, 401K Plan, Performance Bonus, College Fund, Student Loan Refinancing. Salary Range: $120,000-$130,000 a year Qualifications: BACHELOR OF COMPUTER SCIENCE

jobs.title: People Data Engineer
jobs.description: Job Title: HR Data Engineer Location: Onsite Role in San Jose, CA Duration: 06+ Months Contract with Possible Extension Pay Range: $65- $68 Per Hour on W2 Ideal Candidate: The ideal candidate is a hands-on data engineer who can build scalable data architectures and transform messy HR data into clean, self-serve datasets. Day to day, they will write SQL/Python scripts, build dimensional models in Snowflake, and use dbt to support our analytics tool s. Culturally, we need someone who is scrappy, highly collaborative, and comfortable with ambiguity. The perfect fit prioritizes getting things done and making a business impact over rigid perfectionism . Must Hav esSnowflake, SQL, & Python : Expert, hands on experience with Snowflake (including performance tuning and partitioning), plus strong SQL and Python skill s.dbt & Data Modelin g: Proven experience building data pipelines and dimensional models using db t.3+ Years Data Engineerin g: Solid background in data or analytics engineering, with experience modeling data for visualization tools (like Tableau, Looker, or Visier ).HR/People Data Experience : Mandatory prior experience working specifically with HR or People data, alongside the ability to navigate, clean, and structure incomplete or ambiguous dataset s.Scrappy & Pragmatic (Soft Skill ): A flexible, results oriented mindset that focuses on rapid delivery and tangible impact over perfectio n. Job Bri ef:This role sits at the intersection of data engineering and People analytics. You'll build the technical foundation that powers insights about the employee life cycle from hiring to exit while actively experimenting with AI to improve the efficiency of our data infrastructu re.As a member of the people analytics team, you'll design scalable data architectures, optimize our analytics infrastructure, and transform raw data into trusted, reusable datasets. But you'll also need to be scrappy. We value creativity and pragmatism alongside technical excellen ce.This role offers the opportunity to make a significant impact on how we attract, develop, and retain the exceptional talent needed as we are scaling as a compa ny. Responsibilit ies:Data Infrastructure & Mode ling• Create a scalable data foundation that supports visualization tools such as Tableau or HR analytics tools such as Visier platform, and enables strategic insights across the comp any.• Design scalable data architectures and build dimensional models that transform raw data into trusted, reusable datasets for self-serve analytics while maintaining perform ance• Model downstream tables for custom reporting and analy tics• Partner with IT to implement data governance including documentation, lineage tracking, quality monitoring, and proactive alerting sys tems• Develop and maintain automated reporting solutions that scale with organizational gr owth Partners hips:• Partner with stakeholders to define and scope people analytics data required needs that align with organizational goals• Work directly with people analytics team members/analysts to understand business challenges and translate them into scalable technical solu tions• Advise on best practices for integrating and analyzing data from various HR systems and external so urces Require ments:• Have 3+ years in data engineering or analytics engineering with experience in people analytics, or related fields• You are an expert in Snowflake or other cloud tools and processes including optimization, partitioning, and performance tuning• Proficient in SQL, Python, and modern tools li ke dbt• Have experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, L ooker)• Have implemented data security and privacy controls in cloud environment (Preferred not req uired)• You are comfortable working with messy or incomplet e data• You are creative and scrappy, results-oriented with a bias towards flexibility and impact over perf ection

jobs.title: 10822 Power BI Specialist
jobs.description: Barcel USA is looking for a Power BI Specialist to join our growing technology team. The candidate must have professional experience converting data from a multitude of sources into meaningful measures and key performance indicators into interactive, intuitive dashboards, and reports. This position will serve as the Power BI platform subject matter expert. Must be proactive to find opportunities regarding delivering informative and value driven insights with proficiency in statistics and mathematics. This position requires a high level of analytical expertise. The PBI Specialist is responsible for building, administering, and maintaining Power BI workspaces, reports, dashboards, and applications (25%), validating datasets (25%), collaborate with functional and technical teams to implement and improve data analytics to advance informed decision-making (30%), document data models, pipelines, processes, functionality, reports, dashboards, and solutions (15%), and training for Power BI on both dashboards, reports, and technical how-to(s) (5%). Responsibilities Understand/develop business requirements, specifications, and design data models and transform processes to convert data into informative and accurate insights published through reports, dashboards, BI Applications, and other embedded delivery platforms with required functionality for multiple departments including but not limited to Sales, Forecasting, Supply Chain & Finance. Validate data, metrics, KPI against authoritative systems and stake holder expectations. Present and explain to users how to utilize reports, dashboards, applications and how the output is derived via data sources, transformations, logic, and filters. Identify KPIs, measures, and calculations that drive business and monitor them both for data delivery and notify functional team to act. Develop source & validation queries utilizing advanced to Intermediate SQL syntax. Develop well designed reports with clean intuitive interfaces. Promote and evolve the focus of BI analytics. Document data and processes including but not limited to algorithms, parameters, models, metrics, meta data, and transformations. Connecting and troubleshooting data sources, importing data, and transforming data for Business intelligence. Improving existing datasets & models, constructing new datasets/models that are easy to understand in order to promote the reusage of the data and facilitate the self-service analytics. Data & root cause analysis across a number of domains (sales/supply chain/accounting)/ at multiple dimensions and hierarchy levels. Maintain Security via Workspaces, managed dataset permissions, sensitivity labeling, data source/privacy settings and dynamic RLS. Develop tabular and multidimensional models that are compatible with data warehouse standards. Administer workspaces & user access to PBI applications/reports/models and build permissions Any other duties as assigned. Qualifications Understand/develop business requirements, specifications, and design data models and transform processes to convert data into informative and accurate insights published through reports, dashboards, BI Applications, and other embedded delivery platforms with required functionality for multiple departments including but not limited to Sales, Forecasting, Supply Chain & Finance. Validate data, metrics, KPI against authoritative systems and stake holder expectations. Present and explain to users how to utilize reports, dashboards, applications and how the output is derived via data sources, transformations, logic, and filters. Identify KPIs, measures, and calculations that drive business and monitor them both for data delivery and notify functional team to act. Develop source & validation queries utilizing Advances to Intermediate SQL syntax. Develop well designed reports with clean intuitive interfaces. Promote and evolve the focus of BI analytics. Document data and processes including but not limited to algorithms, parameters, models, metrics, meta data, and transformations. Connecting and troubleshooting data sources, importing data, and transforming data for Business intelligence. Improving existing datasets & models, constructing new datasets/models that are easy to understand in order to promote the reusage of the data and facilitate the self-service analytics. Data & root cause analysis across a number of domains (sales/supply chain/accounting)/ at multiple dimensions and hierarchy levels. Maintain Security via Workspaces, managed dataset permissions, sensitivity labeling, data source/privacy settings and dynamic RLS. Develop tabular and multidimensional models that are compatible with data warehouse standards. Administer workspaces & user access to PBI applications/reports/models and build permissions Any other duties as assigned. Education/Experience/Knowledge BS or MS in information systems, data analytics, statistics, computer science, related business field or proven work-related experience. Required Demonstratable Work Experience with Power BI Report Development (Certification: PL-300 / DA-100 desired). 2+ years of experience working with and delivering interactive reporting and analysis solutions utilizing the Microsoft & Azure BI stack. Excellent verbal, written, organizational, and professional presentation skills in group settings to convey complex concepts and technical materials to business function team members. Advanced knowledge of DAX particularly utilizing group calculations, field parameters, and row context understanding. Strong knowledge of business intelligence, data modeling, visualizations, data warehousing, ETL/ELT, data integration, information security practices, and presentation concepts Strong to advanced technical knowledge of SQL (TSQL/MySQL/Databricks-SQL), Power Query-M/DAX, File format data extraction) extraction JSON, XML . Knowledge and utilization of Power Automate, Dataverse, & Writeback Capabilities with Power Apps. API REST/SOAP, Databricks, CSS, PBI SSRS, ADF/SSIS, ETL,. Work experience and domain knowledge in a data driven business setting which may be one of the following: but not isolated to (CPG Sales, Accounting, Finance, Supply Chain, Planning/Forecasting). Barcel USA LLC is an equal opportunity employer with a policy that provides equal employment opportunity for applicants and employees regardless of race, color, religion, disability, gender, age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, pregnancy, veteran status, or any other classification protected by law. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including recruitment, hiring, promotion, compensation, reassignment, layoff, discharge, education, training, and all other working conditions. We believe diversity drives innovation and success. We proudly welcome applications from individuals with disabilities, veterans, and women, and are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description: Job Title: Data Analyst Location: Atlanta, GA | NYC, NY | Remote, USA Employment Type: Full-time, Salaried Who We Are Impiricus is the first and only AI-powered HCP Engagement Engine. In 2025, Deloitte named Impiricus the #1 fastest growing company in North America for their prestigious Fast 500 list. Founded by a practicing physician and a senior pharmaceutical executive, Impiricus was created to transform how life sciences companies support physicians. We ethically connect HCPs to pharma resources, reduce go-to-market costs and accelerate patient access to the treatments they need. With our unique access to the largest opted-in network of HCPs, their insights, and clinical expertise, we are the leading provider of AI technology and real-time channels that life science companies need to deliver clear, reliable, and evidence-based resources directly into the hands of HCPs. Guided by a council of 2000+ trusted HCP advisors, we ensure every interaction is clinically meaningful, ethically grounded and leads to better patient care. Job Summary: As a commercial Data Analyst at Impiricus, you will play a vital role in driving data-informed decision-making across our commercial operations. You will be responsible for analyzing campaign performance, supporting client-facing teams with actionable insights. This position combines analytical expertise with business acumen to deliver valuable insights that enhance our client relationships and drive business growth. Responsibilities: Campaign Performance Analysis & Insights Analyze campaign performance to identify trends, measure effectiveness, and deliver actionable recommendations. Translate complex data into clear business narratives that support commercial strategy and client success. Develop dashboards and recurring reports to monitor key performance metrics and drive data-informed decision-making. Commercial Analytics Partner with Client Services and Sales to provide analytical support for client conversations, quarterly business reviews (QBRs), and commercial initiatives. Analyze cross-campaign and cross-brand performance to uncover insights that inform business development and growth strategies. Leverage internal and external data sources to generate recommendations that improve commercial outcomes. Data Management & Reporting Build, validate, and optimize target lists while ensuring data quality and reporting accuracy. Create impactful visualizations and reports using business intelligence tools for technical and non-technical stakeholders. Identify opportunities to improve reporting processes through automation, AI-enabled tools, and continuous process improvements. Experience: Bachelor's degree, STEM field preferred 1–3 years of experience in data analytics, business analytics, marketing analytics, decision science, or a related analytical role in a consulting or agency setting. Solid experience using data visualization tools such as Sigma, Tableau, Power BI, or similar BI platforms. Strong SQL skills, including the ability to understand and interpret complex queries, joins, and analytical outputs. Experience working with Excel or Google Sheets for data analysis. Familiarity with AI-assisted analytics, workflow automation, or modern productivity tools that enhance reporting and data analysis. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail. Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate data into actionable business insights. Ability to manage competing priorities while collaborating effectively with cross-functional teams and stakeholders. Healthcare, health-tech, or pharmaceutical experience preferred. The base salary range for this role is $85,000 - $95,000. Where you land within the range will reflect your skills, experience, and location, while keeping team parity in mind and leaving room for future growth. Benefits: Impiricus focuses on taking care of our teammates’ professional and personal growth and well-being. Healthcare : Medical, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents + on-demand healthcare concierge HSA, FSA & DCFSA : Pre-tax savings options for healthcare and dependent care, with monthly employer contributions to HSA (if enrolled in a high-deductible plan) Coverage & Protection : 100% paid short- and long-term disability, plus life and AD&D insurance Flexible Time Off : Take the time you need with a flexible vacation policy — recharge your batteries your way Parental Leave : Parental Leave : Paid parental leave to spend time with your newborn, adopted, or foster child (available after 6 months). Your Work, Your Way : If you’re close to an office, we encourage spending some time in-person to collaborate and connect. If not, remote is always an option. Home Office Setup : We’ll ship you the gear you need to create a comfortable workspace at home. 401(k) : Save for your future with tax advantages (and company match!) Impiricus provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

jobs.title: Data Engineer
jobs.description: Company Description Relativix is a vehicle intelligence platform that connects fleet operators and repair shops through shared, high-resolution diagnostic data. The company continuously ingests detailed vehicle information from internal CAN networks and uses predictive analytics to provide clear, evidence-based root-cause insights before vehicles reach the shop. This approach reduces diagnostic guesswork, shortens repair cycles, and improves technician productivity by supplying full historical context instead of isolated error codes. Built as a hardware-enabled, cloud-native platform, Relativix supports modern integrations and agentic AI workflows. The platform helps repair shops work faster and smarter while enabling fleets to minimize downtime, lower maintenance costs, and keep vehicles on the road. Role Description This is a full-time remote role for a Data Engineer. The Data Engineer will design, build, and maintain large-scale data pipelines on the Databricks platform that ingest vehicle telemetry and diagnostic data from diverse sources into Relativix's cloud-native platform on AWS. Day-to-day responsibilities include implementing and optimizing ETL processes at scale, developing and refining data models, and supporting data warehousing and lakehouse solutions to ensure high-quality, reliable datasets for analytics, machine learning, and AI workflows. The role involves collaborating closely with software engineers, data scientists, and product teams to deliver performant data infrastructure, prepare and serve data for machine learning models, and enable advanced predictive capabilities. The Data Engineer will also contribute to monitoring, troubleshooting, and continuously improving data systems to support fleet and repair shop customers. Who You Are We are looking for someone who is passionate about early-stage startups and wants true ownership of what they build. You will work directly with the leadership team to shape and ship cutting-edge products, not execute someone else's spec. Relativix is a flat organization where everyone, including leadership, is a hands-on individual technical contributor. If you want your work to define the foundation of the product and the company, this role is for you. Compensation This position is equity-based to start. Once our current funding round closes, it will convert to a salaried position with competitive compensation. This is an opportunity to join at the ground floor with meaningful ownership in the company. Qualifications Solid, hands-on understanding of the Databricks platform, including Spark, Delta Lake, and lakehouse architecture for large-scale data processing. Proven experience designing, running, and optimizing large-scale ETL pipelines, including data ingestion, transformation, and integration from multiple sources. Strong data engineering fundamentals, including building and maintaining scalable data pipelines and distributed data systems. Solid understanding of AWS and its data services (e.g., S3, Glue, Kinesis, Redshift, Lambda) for building production data infrastructure. Good understanding of machine learning models and their data requirements, including feature engineering, training/serving data pipelines, and supporting data scientists in deploying models to production. Experience with data modeling and data warehousing to design robust schemas and storage solutions for analytics and reporting. Proficiency in a modern programming language commonly used for data engineering (e.g., Python, Scala, or Java) and strong SQL skills. Understanding of best practices in data quality, data governance, and security for production environments. Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. A self-starter mentality with the drive to own projects end-to-end and thrive in the ambiguity of an early-stage startup. Ability to work independently in a remote environment, communicate clearly with cross-functional teams, and manage priorities in a fast-paced setting. Experience with IoT data manipulation, vehicle telemetry, automotive data, or real-time streaming analytics (e.g., Kafka, Kinesis, Spark Structured Streaming) is a strong plus.

jobs.title: UPS Engineer (Data Center)
jobs.description: HIRING: UPS Engineer – Data Center Market Houston Area TX | Remote Working $75K-$110K DOE + Company Vehicle We are looking for an experienced UPS Engineer to join a growing team supporting some of the most critical data center and mission-critical infrastructure projects across the Houston area. This is an opportunity to work with cutting-edge power protection technologies supporting hyperscale, enterprise, colocation, and AI-driven facilities where uptime is everything. What You’ll Be Working On: • Installation, commissioning, maintenance, and troubleshooting of UPS systems • Supporting critical power infrastructure within enterprise and hyperscale data centers • Working with switchgear, batteries, PDUs, STS systems, and backup power environments • Preventative maintenance and emergency response support • Collaborating with engineering, operations, and customer teams to ensure maximum uptime • Supporting both retrofit and greenfield projects across mission-critical facilities Ideal Background: • Experience working with UPS systems in mission-critical environments • Strong electrical or electro-mechanical knowledge • Data center, critical power, or industrial power experience preferred • Familiarity with major OEMs such as Vertiv, Schneider Electric, Eaton, Mitsubishi, ABB, or similar • Ability to travel regionally for customer support and project work • Excellent troubleshooting and customer-facing communication skills Why Join? • Fast-growing critical power company driven by AI and hyperscale data center expansion • Career progression opportunities within a high-growth industry • Exposure to cutting-edge infrastructure technologies • Strong compensation packages including company vehicle • Opportunity to work on some of the largest mission-critical projects in North America If you’re interested in discussing the opportunity confidentially, send me a direct message or apply below: matthew.owen@crossleyscott.com
